entries.question: Who is Elizabeth and what is QLL Counselling?
entries.answer: QLL Counselling & Wellness Practice is run by Elizabeth, a registered counsellor with the Allied Health Professions Council of South Africa (ASCHP). Elizabeth works with individuals navigating stress, anxiety, trauma, grief, and life transitions. Her approach is holistic, combining talk therapy with complementary wellness modalities to support mind, body, and spirit.

entries.question: What services does QLL offer?
entries.answer: QLL offers individual counselling sessions, Rife Therapy, Reiki energy healing, and Ear Acupuncture (auriculotherapy). Sessions can be booked individually or combined as part of a holistic wellness plan. Elizabeth will recommend the most suitable approach after an initial consultation.

entries.question: Who is Elizabeth and what is QLL Counselling?
entries.answer: QLL Counselling & Wellness Practice is run by Elizabeth, a registered counsellor with the Allied Health Professions Council of South Africa (ASCHP). Elizabeth works with individuals navigating stress, anxiety, trauma, grief, and life transitions. Her approach is holistic, combining talk therapy with complementary wellness modalities to support mind, body, and spirit.

entries.question: What services does QLL offer?
entries.answer: QLL offers individual counselling sessions, Rife Therapy, Reiki energy healing, and Ear Acupuncture (auriculotherapy). Sessions can be booked individually or combined as part of a holistic wellness plan. Elizabeth will recommend the most suitable approach after an initial consultation.

entries.question: What is counselling and what does it help with?
entries.answer: Counselling at QLL is a confidential, supportive space to explore thoughts, feelings, and challenges. Elizabeth works with clients experiencing anxiety, stress, depression, trauma, grief, relationship difficulties, and major life changes. Sessions are non-judgmental and client-led — you set the pace.

entries.question: What is Rife Therapy?
entries.answer: Rife Therapy uses specific electromagnetic frequencies to support the body's natural healing processes. It is a complementary (not medical) treatment used to support relaxation, energy balance, and general wellness. It is not a substitute for medical treatment and Elizabeth will always advise clients to consult a medical doctor for any medical concerns.

entries.question: What is Reiki?
entries.answer: Reiki is a gentle energy healing practice where the practitioner channels universal life energy to promote relaxation, stress reduction, and emotional balance. Sessions are fully clothed and non-invasive. Many clients use Reiki alongside counselling to support emotional processing and overall wellbeing.

entries.question: What is Ear Acupuncture?
entries.answer: Ear Acupuncture, or auriculotherapy, involves stimulating specific points on the ear to support relaxation, reduce cravings, manage pain, and improve emotional wellbeing. Fine, sterile needles or non-invasive pressure tools are used. It is a complementary treatment and not a replacement for medical care.

entries.question: How much does a session cost?
entries.answer: Please contact Elizabeth directly for current session pricing, as rates may vary depending on the service and session length. An initial consultation is available to discuss your needs before committing to a full session. Pricing information will be confirmed at booking.

entries.question: Do you accept medical aid?
entries.answer: Please contact Elizabeth directly to confirm current medical aid options, as this depends on your specific scheme and plan. Some ASCHP-registered practitioners are covered under certain wellness benefits — it is worth checking with your medical aid provider directly.

entries.question: How do I book a session?
entries.answer: You can book a session by contacting Elizabeth directly via phone, WhatsApp, or email. She will confirm availability, discuss which service is right for you, and send you all the details you need ahead of your appointment. Contact details are available on the QLL website.

entries.question: What is the cancellation policy?
entries.answer: Please contact Elizabeth as soon as possible if you need to cancel or reschedule. A minimum notice period applies — Elizabeth will confirm the exact policy at the time of booking. Late cancellations or no-shows may be subject to a fee.

entries.question: Are sessions available online or only in person?
entries.answer: QLL offers both in-person sessions at the Pretoria practice and online sessions via video call. Rife Therapy and Ear Acupuncture require an in-person visit, while counselling and Reiki may be available online. Please confirm with Elizabeth at the time of booking.

entries.question: Where is QLL located and is there parking?
entries.answer: QLL Counselling & Wellness Practice is located in Pretoria, South Africa. Parking is available on site. Please contact Elizabeth for the exact address and directions, which will be confirmed at the time of your booking.

entries.question: What can I expect from my first session?
entries.answer: Your first session is primarily a consultation — a relaxed conversation for Elizabeth to understand your needs, answer your questions, and explain how she can help. There is no pressure to share more than you are comfortable with. You will leave with a clear sense of the recommended approach and next steps.

entries.question: Is everything I share confidential?
entries.answer: Yes. All sessions at QLL are strictly confidential. Elizabeth operates under the ethical guidelines of the ASCHP and South African privacy law (POPIA). Information is only shared without consent in very limited circumstances — where there is a serious and imminent risk of harm to you or others. This will always be discussed with you transparently.

entries.question: Is Elizabeth a medical doctor or psychologist?
entries.answer: No. Elizabeth is a registered counsellor with the ASCHP, not a medical doctor, psychiatrist, or psychologist. She provides supportive counselling and complementary wellness therapies. For medical diagnoses, medication management, or psychiatric assessment, please consult a medical doctor or registered psychologist.

crisis.response:
I can hear that you're going through something very painful right now. Please reach out to one of these free, confidential helplines immediately — trained counsellors are available 24/7:

• SADAG (South African Depression & Anxiety Group): 0800 456 789 (toll-free, 24/7)
• Suicide Crisis Line: 0800 567 567 (toll-free, 24/7)
• Lifeline South Africa: 0861 322 322
• Gender-Based Violence Command Centre: 0800 428 428 (toll-free, 24/7)

If you are in immediate danger, please call 10111 (SAPS) or 10177 (ambulance).

Elizabeth at QLL is here to support your wellness journey, but these helplines are equipped to help you right now.

guardrails.no_diagnosis_response: That's an important question, but diagnosing mental health conditions is outside the scope of what I can help with — and it wouldn't be responsible for me to guess. What I can tell you is that Elizabeth offers a safe space to explore what you're experiencing, and she can refer you to the right professional if a formal assessment is needed. Would you like to know how to book an initial consultation?
